records.ety: [L. exhibitus, p. p. of exhibere to hold forth, to tender, exhibit; ex out + habere to have or hold. See Habit.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exhibited; p. pr. & vb. n. Exhibiting.]

defs: To hold forth or present to view; to produce publicly, for inspection; to show, especially in order to attract notice to what is interesting; to display; as, to exhibit commodities in a warehouse, a picture in a gallery.
quotes: Exhibiting a miserable example of the weakness of mind and body.

defs: To submit, as a document, to a court or officer, in course of proceedings; also, to present or offer officially or in legal form; to bring, as a charge.
quotes: He suffered his attorney-general to exhibit a charge of high treason against the earl.

defs: To administer as a remedy; as, to exhibit calomel.
Any article, or collection of articles, displayed to view, as in an industrial exhibition; a display; as, this exhibit was marked A; the English exhibit.
A document produced and identified in court for future use as evidence.
